PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Espey for Mary Jo Reliford (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it has no problem with the Wells Fargo account, that it seems appropriate, that it was established during the marriage and that it should be treated as community property. The Court also stated that the real property was quite intentionally deeded outside to the husband as separate property and done on the representation that this was the parties' intent, and that there is no writing treating it as community property. Ms. Espey stated that the deed was prepared by a title company as far as financing, that the parties were not aware of all of the ramifications of it and that there was no intention other than financing. The Court noted that they would not have gotten financing if the Wife had not relinquished her interest. Ms. Espey stated that this is common in lending to remove someone from title, that they certainly built up a community interest, that they used community funds to pay the mortgage and that all of the rental income was filed on their tax returns. Ms. Espey also stated that they were married for 51 years, that it was an investment property and that they both enjoyed income from the property. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Spousal Property as to the bank account, and denied the petition as to the real property stating that it will need to be probated. Ms. Espey was advised to use the same case number when filing her petition for probate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
